records.ety: [Corrupted (by influence of lieu place) fr. OF. purale, porale (equiv. to LL. perambulatio a survey of boundaries, originally, a going through); por (L. pro, confused, however, with L. per through) + ale. See Pro-, and Alley.]

defs: Originally, the ground near a royal forest, which, having been unlawfully added to the forest, was afterwards severed from it, and disafforested so as to remit to the former owners their rights.
quotes: Then as a tiger, who by chance hath spied In some purlieu two gentle fawns at play.

defs: Hence, the outer portion of any place; an adjacent district; environs; neighborhood.
quotes: brokers had been incessantly plying for custom in the purlieus of the court.
